title: What to pack for Summer Camp at Camp Dream GA
excerpt: A practical packing guide for families who want to feel ready without overpacking or missing the basics that matter most.

sections.heading: Start with comfort and routine basics
sections.body: The most helpful packing list usually begins with comfortable clothing, weather-appropriate layers, toiletries, sleep essentials, and any routine items a camper depends on.

sections.heading: Pack for flexibility instead of perfection
sections.body: Georgia weather can shift quickly and camp days can get active. Extra socks, a light rain layer, labeled items, and simple comfort pieces often matter more than packing a long list of gear.

sections.heading: Use packing as a confidence tool
sections.body: A clear packing list helps families feel less rushed and gives campers a calmer transition into the summer experience.

cta.heading: Use the FAQ and location pages if you want the rest of the practical camp-prep details in one place.
cta.body: The resource section is here to reduce stress and make decisions easier for families.
